info.name: Act-One Ticketing
info.description:
Act-One Ticketing API collection for managing tickets, transactions, auditoriums, donations, and feedback.

Getting started
- Set the active environment to Act One and ensure these variables are defined:
  - baseUrl (e.g., http://localhost:8080)
  - admin-password (string)
- Requests inherit baseUrl via {{baseUrl}} and any admin password usage should reference {{admin-password}}.

Notes
- Collection-level pre-request script generates a uniqueEmail for test runs.
- All localhost URLs have been normalized to use {{baseUrl}}.

request.description:
Checks in a ticket by its `ticketId`.

Purpose:
- Mark a ticket as used/checked-in.

Path params:
- `ticketId` – UUID of the ticket, stored as a collection variable from the Issue Ticket request.

Expected success response (first check-in):
- 200 OK with JSON body indicating success. Common patterns:
  - `{ "status": "USED" }`
  - `{ "result": "VALID" }`

Expected duplicate / idempotency behavior (second check-in):
- 200 OK, 400, or 409 (all treated as acceptable).
- Body indicates that ticket is already used / invalid for re-entry (e.g. `ALREADY_USED`, `INVALID`, etc.).

Error cases:
- 400 / 404 / 409 depending on implementation if ticket does not exist or is in an invalid state.

Fetches a ticket by its `ticketId`.

Purpose:
- Retrieve ticket details after issuing.

Path params:
- `ticketId` – UUID of the ticket. Stored as a collection variable by the Issue Ticket request.

Expected success responses:
- 200 OK with JSON body containing at least `ticketId`.

Error cases:
- 400 / 404 / 409 depending on implementation if ticket does not exist or id is invalid.

Notes:
- This endpoint is inferred from common Spring conventions and may not be implemented exactly as `/api/tickets/{ticketId}` in your service.

exec: // Tests tab visualization to parse ZIP responses and chart file sizes
// Detect ZIP via Content-Type or PK header, parse entries with JSZip, then render Chart.js bar chart
Showing first 50 files, remaining aggregated as "Others".
Response is not a ZIP file. Content-Type: {{meta.contentType}}
tooltips: { callbacks: { label: function(tooltipItem, d){ return d.labels[tooltipItem.index] + ': ' + sizes[tooltipItem.index] + ' KB'; } } }
var meta = { isZip: false, contentType: '', totalFiles: 0, totalSizeKB: 0, truncated: false };
if (!detection.isZip) { return { meta: meta, chart: chart }; }
// Load JSZip from CDN, then parse the binary
return resolve({ meta: meta, chart: chart });
return resolve({ meta: meta, chart: chart });
entries.push({ name: relativePath, size: file._data ? (file._data.uncompressedSize || 0) : file._dataUncompressedSize || 0 });
// JSZip doesn't expose sizes until async content is read in some cases; fallback by collecting via file._data if available
// If size unknown, set 0 and it will still render
entries = entries.map(function(e){ return { name: e.name, sizeKB: Math.round((e.size || 0) / 1024) }; });
var totalSizeKB = entries.reduce(function(a, b){ return a + (b.sizeKB || 0); }, 0);

// Detect ZIP via Content-Type or PK header, parse entries with JSZip, then render Chart.js bar chart
Showing first 50 files, remaining aggregated as "Others".
Response is not a ZIP file. Content-Type: {{meta.contentType}}
tooltips: { callbacks: { label: function(tooltipItem, d){ return d.labels[tooltipItem.index] + ': ' + sizes[tooltipItem.index] + ' KB'; } } }
var meta = { isZip: false, contentType: '', totalFiles: 0, totalSizeKB: 0, truncated: false };
if (!detection.isZip) { return { meta: meta, chart: chart }; }
// Load JSZip from CDN, then parse the binary
return resolve({ meta: meta, chart: chart });
return resolve({ meta: meta, chart: chart });
entries.push({ name: relativePath, size: file._data ? (file._data.uncompressedSize || 0) : file._dataUncompressedSize || 0 });
// JSZip doesn't expose sizes until async content is read in some cases; fallback by collecting via file._data if available
// If size unknown, set 0 and it will still render
entries = entries.map(function(e){ return { name: e.name, sizeKB: Math.round((e.size || 0) / 1024) }; });
var totalSizeKB = entries.reduce(function(a, b){ return a + (b.sizeKB || 0); }, 0);

request.description:
Issues a new ticket for a show.

Purpose:
- Create a new ticket with show and attendee details.

Request body (JSON):
```json
{
  "showId": null,
  "showName": "Act One — Opening Night",
  "fullName": "Aisha Khan",
  "email": "{{uniqueEmail}}",
  "phoneNumber": "+44 7700 900101"
}
```

Expected success responses:
- 200 OK or 201 Created.
- JSON body containing at minimum: `ticketId` (UUID).
- Optionally may contain `barcodeId` (UUID) and `status` (e.g. `ISSUED`).

Error cases:
- 400 Bad Request for validation errors (missing/invalid fields).

Generated from cURL: curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/transactions/record -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"showName":"Evening Show","showId":"SHOW-1","fullName":"Alex Doe","email":"alex@example.com","phoneNumber":"+15550001111","ticketCount":2,"ticketAmount":1500,"transactionId":"TXN-9001"}'

Generated from cURL: curl --location 'http://localhost:8080/api/transactions/record-pending' --header 'Content-Type: application/json' --data '{"showId":null,"showName":"Test Show","fullName":"Rishabh Kar","email":"rishabh.kar.ai@gmail.com","phoneNumber":"+919916604905","ticketCount":2,"transactionId":"TXN-CASH-1","ticketAmount":"500.00"}'
Generated from cURL: curl --location 'http://localhost:8080/api/transactions/by-phone?phoneNumber=9916604905'
Generated from cURL: curl --location 'http://localhost:8080/api/transactions/by-name?fullName=Rishabh'

pm.test('Response has JSON content-type (if provided)', function () {
// Parse JSON safely and validate shape
pm.test('Response body is valid JSON', function () {
// Even if header missing, parsing should succeed for valid JSON
// If parsing fails, mark test as failed explicitly
pm.expect.fail('Response body is not valid JSON: ' + e.message);
// Ensure required fields exist and are strings
pm.test('Response has showId and auditoriumId as strings', function () {
// Store values into environment variables if valid
pm.test('Store showId and auditoriumId to environment', function () {
if (data && typeof data === 'object' && typeof data.showId === 'string' && typeof data.auditoriumId === 'string') {
pm.expect.fail('showId or auditoriumId missing or not strings; not setting environment variables.');
// Presence checks for other typical fields if present (non-strict)
pm.test('Optional fields have expected types when present', function () {
if (Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(data, k) && data[k] !== null && data[k] !== undefined) {
if (Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(data, k) && data[k] !== null && data[k] !== undefined) {
pm.expect(data[k]).to.satisfy(n => typeof n === 'number' && isFinite(n), k + ' should be a finite number');
